title: Level 507
wikitext:
''{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 507: "Beyond This World"}}' {{Background|file=Balcon-and-on-and-on-and-on-and-on-and-ony-v0-9vh09rs5otqe1.webp}}
{{Class 5|hexagon=γ|color-h=196|color-s=38%|color-l=28%|class=Gamma|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Gravitational Hazards}} '''Level 507 is the 508th level of the Backrooms.''' ==Description== {{Blurimage|caption=The first image of Level 507.|file=Balcon-and-on-and-on-and-on-and-on-and-ony-v0-9vh09rs5otqe1.webp}} Level 507, better known as "Beyond this World", is a labyrinth of red and orange balconies. Forward is an infinite row of balconies, and looking out from them is an endless blue sky. The level seems to be in a permanent state of summer, as the level is hot all the time. The level poses severe gravitational hazards to the wanderer present. The balcony of the level is presumed to be 7 square feet. Each balcony has its own idiosyncrasies. For example, some balconies have 3 doors or have infinitely tall vertical structures. The floor of the balcony is a cold concrete with a distinct beige color. Ripping open the concrete will lead to [[The Void]]. At the baseboard of the wall is an uncomfortable "Welcome Home!" mug. The coir fibers are old and damp. The rug has the distinct patterns of green and pink flowers. At the bottom of the rug is a big text saying a random phrase. The rug seems to be permanently stuck to the ground by some sticky liquid. It is still unknown what this is. The plant on the table is very confusing to most wanderers. The plant requires human care, or you will not be able to exit the level. The plant needs to be watered twice a day, put in the sun for only 3 hours and 17 minutes, and repainted twice a week. The plant is theorized to be some sort of an [[Object 1|Almond water]] plant. This is because of the distinct smell of almonds. Wanderers report eating the plant in desperate times, leading to them being healed. Some benefits of the plant are sharper reaction time and maintained sanity. The flowerpot is extremely small for a plant, being the size of a small glass cup. It is painted with a striped blue and yellow pattern. Painting it in the wrong way will force you to find another balcony and start over. The walls of the room are always a distinct orange color. The walls seemed to be made of concrete due to its hard properties and resistance to heat. The walls are around 15 feet tall, giving a lot of leverage for the wanderer. The walls have two windows, one looking out to other balconies and one looking out to an infinite sky. Each window is around 5 square feet and is around 10 inches thick. There is railing in the smaller portion of each window. These are intersecting black lines with a stick of wood at the top of them. At the other side of the room is a door. This presumably leads to the inside, although it seems to be fixed to the wall. The door is around the size of the wall and is a dark beige. The window is bulletproof, and there is a bright light behind the blue curtains covering it. This is theorized to be an exit to [[The Empty]], as it shows similar characteristics presented by the inside of the building. The roof is very unstable. The roof is a dark beige, similar to the floor of the balcony. In the middle of it is a decently sized fan. The fan has 5 wooden blades and 4 lights in the middle. The blades are extremely unrefined and sharp. They spin extremely fast, around 150 rpm. The dust on top of them is toxic to humans, so it is recommended not to interact with it. The lights are covered by a checkered gold railing. In the middle are 4 outputting lights. Oddly enough, only 3 out of the 4 lights work. The other one is shattered and broken. The level poses a severe hazard of gravitational anomalies. The wanderer will always float around 1 inch up from the ground. Jumping in the level brings a max jump height of around 7 feet. Wanderers also report slowed fall time after reaching the apex of their jump. This hazard has puzzled theorists for centuries. It is still unknown what causes this, and MEG has had multiple experiments operated on figuring out this issue. ==The Stairs== {{Blurimage|align=left|caption=The infinite stairs..|file=Level507.jpg}} The stairs in the level are extremely hard to access. The stairs can be accessed by going far enough into the void. After going around 1,000 feet, the wanderer can noclip into the landscape of the infinite sky. It will take around 1 minute for the wanderers to be transported to the area. The wanderer will be placed in a large white room. After opening the door, the wanderer will be in the stair section. The stair section is described as having hot pink walls with grey stairs. The stairs feel ceramic and are extremely smooth. They are painted a daunting light grey color similar to the walls of level zero. Wanderers report the stairs being 2 feet wide and 10 feet across. There is presumed to be 7 stairs per each staircase. Next to the stairs are 3-foot-tall thick pink walls. They pose similar qualities to the staircases. The walls of this section stretch on for infinity. They are very rough, poorly painted, thick pink walls. Wanderers report hearing screaming coming through the wall, although there is no real recording of this. It is supposed the screaming is from deranged wanderers in levels with psychological hazards. Power outlets can be found on the wall and are far out of human reach. It is supposed that electricity can be found in them, although this is yet to be proven. Windows can also be found on the wall. They are around one foot out and are extremely smooth. The blinds are a dark beige color. Looking inside them is also supposed to be another glance at [[The Empty]] It is recommended not to touch the light, as it will reset your progress growing the plant. To get back into the balconies, the wanderer can no-clip through the dark red parts of the walls. These can be found under some of the window blinds. The wanderer will be sent into the sky. After walking for around 30 minutes, the wanderer will start to see the expanse of balconies again. Objects in the void can be found, such as trees or benches. Once you get back to the balcony, you will be teleported to the balcony with the plant you are taking care of. ==Entities== There are no entities in the level. ==Colonies and Outposts== The Balcony Club is a group of people who inhabit the staircase area of the level. They decided to live in that area to be freed from the strict routine of the balconies. The have around 15 normal members, 3 head members, and one leader. They are welcoming new wanderers at all times and are willing trade with any group of any new groups emerge. They have a decent supply of items collected from the void or the staircase area. These include [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and weapons. ==Entrances== * Noclipping on the trains from [[Level 503]] will send you here. *Finding a balcony in [[Level 505]] or [[Level 506]]. ==Exits== * Taking care of the plant for 7 days will send you to a random level.
